Definition: Haplosclerida sp. GHC0000330 genome assembly, chromosome: 20
Description: Haplosclerida sp. GHC0000330 genome assembly, chromosome: 20
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Porifera; Demospongiae; Heteroscleromorpha; Haplosclerida
Assembly: The assembly odHalSpea1.1 is based on 284x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Definition: Astatotilapia calliptera genome assembly, chromosome: 1
Description: Astatotilapia calliptera genome assembly, chromosome: 1
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Actinopterygii; Neopterygii; Teleostei; Neoteleostei; Acanthomorphata; Ovalentaria; Cichlomorphae; Cichliformes; Cichlidae; African cichlids; Pseudocrenilabrinae; Haplochromini; Astatotilapia
Assembly: On Jan 17, 2025 this sequence version replaced LS420019.1.; The assembly fAstCal1.5 is based on ~48x PacBio RSII data, ~112x Illumina HiSeqX data generated from a 10X Genomics Chromium library, and single-enzyme BioNano Irys data. Hi-C was obtained from sperm of an A. calliptera individual from 'Mbenji Island? in Lake Malawi. An initial PacBio assembly was made using Falcon-unzip and the primary contigs extended by merging with a miniasm assembly. The contigs were then scaffolded using the 10X data with scaff10x. After using the PacBio data to gap fill with PBJelly and polish with Quiver, the assembly was manually curated using gEVAL to correct mis-joins and improve concordance with the BioNano data. The assembly was then polished again using the 10X Illumina data and freebayes. Two genetic maps [PMID: 23622422, 25156298] were used to allocate scaffolds to chromosomes, followed by further curation and verification using gEVAL. The Hi-C data was used for manual curation using Pretext View. The mitochondrial assembly was produced at The Rockefeller University using mitoVGP.

Definition: Andreaea hookeri genome assembly, chromosome: 7
Description: Andreaea hookeri genome assembly, chromosome: 7
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Viridiplantae; Streptophyta; Embryophyta; Bryophyta; Andreaeophytina; Andreaeopsida; Andreaeales; Andreaeaceae; Andreaea
Assembly: The assembly caAndHook4.1 is based on 60x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Definition: Spinus spinus genome assembly, chromosome: 7
Description: Spinus spinus genome assembly, chromosome: 7
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Archelosauria; Archosauria; Dinosauria; Saurischia; Theropoda; Coelurosauria; Aves; Neognathae; Neoaves; Telluraves; Australaves; Passeriformes; Passeroidea; Fringillidae; Carduelinae; Spinus
Assembly: This project provides the alternate haplotype genome assembly bSpiSpi1.1 of the Eurasian siskin, Spinus spinus. The sample was provided by Jan T. Lifjeld, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). bSpiSpi1.1 is based on 32x PacBio HiFi data and 69x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.

Definition: MAG: Rhodothermales bacterium isolate 9ce07578-5119-4228-8939-1a3e4132b483 genome assembly, chromosome: 1
Description: MAG: Rhodothermales bacterium isolate 9ce07578-5119-4228-8939-1a3e4132b483 genome assembly, chromosome: 1
Taxonomy_y: Bacteria; Pseudomonadati; Rhodothermota; Rhodothermia; Rhodothermales
Assembly: The metagenome-assembled genome assembly odIrcFeli1.Rhodothermales_1.1 is based on 3765x PacBio data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with metaMDBG and coverage-, graph-, and HiC-based contig binning.

Assembly: This project provides the diploid genome assembly bFriCoe2.1 of the Eurasian chaffinch, Fringilla coelebs. The sample was provided by Jan T. Lifjeld, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). bFriCoe2.1 is based on 21x PacBio HiFi data and 67x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.
